title: Clean Fuels Weekly Commentary: Credit Prices Rise WoW Along with Increase in Type 1 Transfer Volumes
content:
This Weekâ€™s Highlights:

(1st â€“ 7th September 2025)
  • A total of 141,405 credits were exchanged as Type 1 trades, a 133.02% significant rise week-over-week (WoW), and all non-zero trades witnessed a rise of 39.32% WoW as 641,405 credits were traded in CA LCFS.
  • The weekly average CA LCFS credit price stood at $56.61/MT for Type 1 transfers, recording a 3.00% rise WoW; All Non-Zero transfers increased by 1.26% in price, reaching $56.85/MT.
  • The average LCFS spot credit price declined by 1.19% WoW; on 9th September, the spot price stood at $54.75/MT, and the benchmark price stood at $56.88/MT.
  • D3 RIN prices remained unchanged WoW. Both D4 and D5 prices fell by 4.72% WoW, while D6 price fell by 6.48% WoW.

cCarbonâ€™s Market View

The California Senate voted unanimously to approve the sales of E15 in the state for the first time. Allowing E15 sales in California opens a significant ethanol market for biofuel producers. The move toward E15 in the state has come at a time when the state has been experiencing an increasing number of oil refinery closures, raising concerns about lower gasoline supply and higher prices. In Canada, PM Carney announced a series of new strategic measures for workers and businesses impacted by U.S. tariffs and trade disruptions including the introduction of a new biofuel production incentive. The Environmental Climate Change Canada (ECCC) has indicated that it would commence the process of making targeted amendments to the Clean Fuel Regulations (CFR). cCarbon will provide comprehensive analysis as additional program details emerge from Natural Resources Canada and Environment and Climate Change Canada in the coming weeks.

CA LCFS Weekly Price and Volume Trend

[caption id="attachment_603665" align="alignnone" width="777"] Fig 1. Weekly average credit prices and weekly volumes, Source: California ARB[/caption] The average Type 1 prices recorded a 3.00% WoW decline, rising from $54.96 to $56.61 this week, and all non-zero transfer prices increased by 1.26% WoW, rising from $56.14 to $56.85. Type 1 trades witnessed 141,405 credits exchanged, reflecting a 133.02% rise WoW. The total transaction volumes for all non-zero trades saw an increase of 39.32% WoW, reaching 641,405 credits.  

CA LCFS Daily Average Spot Price

[caption id="attachment_603667" align="alignnone" width="1000"] Fig 2. California LCFS Weekly average spot prices and benchmark (*CC.Info OTC: Numbers are an average of broker prices reported to CC.info)[/caption] Last week, the average LCFS spot credit prices fell by 1.19% WoW, reaching $55.63/MT. On 9th September, the spot price stood at $54.75/MT. The benchmark price, representing the future price of contracts expiring at the end of 2025, stood at $56.88/MT as of 9th September.

CA LCFS Weekly Trade Frequency and Volume

[caption id="attachment_603668" align="alignnone" width="1000"] Fig 3. Trade frequency and volume, Source: California ARB[/caption] As per the data reported by CARB last week, 40 trades were reported in the market, with 3 high-volume trades. The highest transfer by price was $70.00 for a Type 2 transaction at a volume of 14,000 credits. The lowest transfer by price was $52.50 per credit for one Type 2 transaction reported for 20,000 credits. The largest volume of one transfer involved 210,000 credits for Type 2 transactions priced at $56.60. The smallest volume of transfer involved 500 credits for a Type 2 transaction priced at $57.50.

CA LCFS Monthly Volumes and Price Trend

[caption id="attachment_603669" align="alignnone" width="1000"] Fig 4. Total monthly volumes and associated prices, Source: California ARB[/caption] The average spot Type 1 price stood at $56.61 in September so far, up from $56.47 in August. Trade volumes for the month of September stood at 0.64 M as of 7th September, 2025.

CA LCFS Weekly Volume Price Trend

[caption id="attachment_603670" align="alignnone" width="1000"] Fig 5. Volume Price Trend indicator for the CA LCFS market, Source: California ARB[/caption] The Volume Price Trend Indicator (VPT) helps identify the parity between supply and demand for a stock. The idea behind the indicator is to multiply the market volume of LCFS credits by the percentage change in its price. If the credit price declines, the value of the indicator falls. Across 1st â€“ 7th September, total VPT recorded a rise of 1.07% WoW, with a rise in all non-zero credit prices for the last week. The Volume Price Trend Indicator (VPT) helps identify the parity between supply and demand for a stock. The idea behind the indicator is to multiply the market volume of LCFS credits by the percentage change in its price. If the credit price declines, the value of the indicator falls.

Â Renewable Fuel Standard (RFS): RIN Weekly Price Trend

RINs are credits used for compliance and are the â€œcurrencyâ€ of the RFS program. Renewable fuel producers generate RINs, market participants trade RINs, and obligated parties obtain and then ultimately retire RINs for the compliance list. D3 RIN prices remained unchanged WoW. Both D4 and D5 prices fell by 4.72% WoW, while D6 price fell by 6.48% WoW. D3: Cellulosic biofuel D4: Biomass-based diesel D5: Advanced biofuels (produced from any type of renewable biomass except corn starch ethanol) D6: Conventional renewable biofuel ethanol derived from corn starch, or any other qualifying renewable fuel

Upcoming datesÂ 

Â Key Clean Fuels Market Developments this Week

  • EU Carmakers Demand Flexibility Beyond 2035 Combustion Ban: Europeâ€™s at a crossroads in its quest for industrial decarbonization, and the big wigs at ACEA (thatâ€™s the European Automobile Manufacturersâ€™ Association) and CLEPA (the European Association of Automotive Suppliers) just sounded a pretty stark alarm: the EUâ€™s plan to outlaw new combustion-engine cars by 2035 might be dreaming if it doesnâ€™t reckon with industry realities and geopolitical hurdles.ACEA, based in Brussels, rallies the titans of the car worldâ€”think Mercedes-Benz, BMW and Volkswagenâ€”while CLEPA speaks for more than 3,000 suppliers, from Schaeffler AG to Valeo.
  • California Sets Aside Penalties for High Refinery Profits: Californiaâ€™s Energy Commission voted to temporarily set aside penalties for excessive refining profits that were adopted after gasoline pump prices climbed over $8 a gallon in 2022.Californiaâ€™s Democratic Governor Gavin Newsom had proposed the penalties, but has since switched direction amid worries of price spikes in 2026 after the closure of the Phillips 66 refinery and a San Francisco-area plant operated by Valero Energy Corp next year.Both companies said declining gasoline demand promoted by stateâ€™s policies in favor of non-fossil-fuel-powered vehicles made the once-lucrative California market untenable in the long-term.
  • California Lawmakers Vote to Allow Sale of Higher-Ethanol Fuel: California lawmakers voted to allow the sale of a higher-ethanol gasoline blend that could help bring down fuel prices in the largest U.S. auto market. The sale of E15, a blend containing 15% ethanol, would be allowed immediately once the bill is signed by Governor Gavin Newsom. California had been the only U.S. state that did not allow sales of the fuel."California consumers cannot wait any longer," California Senator Laura Richardson said on the Senate floor while introducing the bill. She pointed to a University of California study that estimated that the fuel's availability could lower gas prices in the state by 20 cents per gallon.
  • ACE Unveils E15 Cost Calculator at CFCA Summit: With California on the cusp of approving sales of E15, the American Coalition for Ethanol (ACE) introduced a powerful new tool this week at the California Fuels + Convenience Alliance (CFCA) Summit to help fuel marketers seize the opportunity: the E15 Cost Calculator, now live at FlexFuelForward.com, ACEâ€™s digital hub tailored to fuel marketers. The online calculator equips retailers with a resource to plug in real-time data to compare the costs and potential profits of selling E15 versus E10.t provides station-specific insights, including applicable state and federal tax credits, to show whether offering E15 can boost the bottom line.
  • Prime Minister Carney Launches New Measures to Protect, Build, and Transform Canadian Strategic Industries, Including the Biofuel Sector: The Prime Minister, Mark Carney announced a series of new strategic measures for workers and businesses in those sectors most impacted by U.S. tariffs and trade disruptions including the introduction of a new biofuel production incentive, with over $370 million for domestic producers to address immediate competitiveness challenges, amend Clean Fuel Regulations to support the domestic biofuels industry, temporarily increase the Advance Payments Program interest-free limit to $500,000 for canola advances. Also, ECCC has mentioned to start the process to make a very targeted amendment of the CFR to address the issues in CFR.
